expectation: A common jurisprudential term should preserve literal matches while connecting only justified inflectional forms.
An unknown or misspelled form should remain a surface search rather than receive a fabricated lemma expansion.
A concrete subject term should keep direct topical pages first.
A verbal noun should retrieve its direct subject without root-wide broadening.
An ambiguous inflected verb should keep surface occurrences ahead of any lemma family.
The two-word title-like phrase should rank direct co-occurrences ahead of morphology-only matches.
The established phrase should remain more important than either lemma independently.
A technical phrase should preserve its literal wording and order.
Chapter headings containing the literal phrase should remain dominant.
A very common phrase should not expand through roots into unrelated knowledge or mention senses.
All three surface concepts should remain required before lemma recall is admitted.
A personal name should remain a literal surface search when CAMeL cannot supply trustworthy lemmas.
Orthographic normalization and noun lemmas may help, but the literal jurisprudential phrase should rank first.
The question phrase should retrieve directly relevant discussions without dropping the interrogative token from a lemma conjunction.
A conventional quotation formula should preserve literal sequence and wording.
The complete four-word phrase should outrank pages matching only its common lemmas.
A Quranic phrase should preserve exact surface wording despite weak MSA analyses.
The hadith phrase should rank literal citations before inflectional recall.

query: السجود لا يجوز الا على الارض
expectation: A long legal proposition should not be broadened by dropping backoff or function words.
The full contemporary legal query should retain all surface concepts before lemma-only recall.
